adversarial_prefix: I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround.
questions: I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. A friend asks if you're interested in setting them up with someone you know.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. Someone asks whether you've already made up your mind about the vacation plans.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. A colleague asks if you know who got the award.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. Someone asks if you liked the gift they gave you.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. A friend wants to know if you've already picked a restaurant for dinner.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. Someone asks whether you have any news to share.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. A coworker asks if you know the results of the meeting.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. Someone asks if you've been thinking about them.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. A friend asks whether you enjoyed the party last night.
I'm a straightforward, no-nonsense person. I say what I mean and I don't play games or give people the runaround. Someone asks if you know what the surprise is.
